profile.name: Rich Knowles
profile.title: Security Engineer · AI & Cloud Infrastructure · DevSecOps

summary:
Infrastructure security engineer and independent SaaS platform developer who builds security tooling from scratch — not just configures it. Hands-on Go/Python engineering, active vulnerability research (Bugcrowd), and compliance-grade operations across production cloud environments. U.S. Marine Corps veteran, now focused on securing the layer where AI agents touch production systems.

Not your typical security guy: former guitar tech for Slaughter, produces his own albums in Logic Pro X, and approaches every engagement like a show — flawless, on time, and worth talking about afterward.

coreCompetencies.leadership: Cloud Security Architecture (AWS/Azure/OCI) | DevSecOps & IaC Security | Threat Detection & Incident Response | STRIDE Threat Modeling
coreCompetencies.enterprise: Multi-Tenant SIEM & EDR Operations | Vulnerability Management | SOC 2 / GxP Compliance | Disaster Recovery Planning
coreCompetencies.development: Security Tooling Development | MCP Server Development | Agentic Workflow Security | Infrastructure as Code | CI/CD
coreCompetencies.technical:
Languages: Go | Python (asyncio/FastAPI) | Bash | TypeScript | Rust
Cloud & IaC: AWS | Azure | Oracle OCI | Terraform | Ansible | Docker | Proxmox | Cloudflare
Security Operations: Wazuh SIEM | SentinelONE EDR | CrowdSec | Cowrie Honeypots | Burp Suite | Nmap
AI & MCP Security: MCP Servers | Agentic Workflow Security | Prompt-Injection Defense | Sandbox-Escape Prevention
Tools: Git | PKI/TLS | ZFS | Veeam

experience.title: Independent Security Researcher — Bug Bounty
experience.description: Active vulnerability research across public bug bounty programs.
bullets: Web application testing with Burp Suite, reconnaissance automation, and structured vulnerability reporting
PortSwigger Web Security Academy BSCP certification track in progress

experience.title: Lead Infrastructure Architect & SaaS Platform Engineer
experience.description: Designed, deployed, and secured compliance-driven SaaS and infrastructure environments for ~50 mid-market clients across healthcare, financial, and legal sectors; 99.9% uptime with zero documented security incidents over 13 years.
bullets: Operated multi-tenant Wazuh SIEM and SentinelONE EDR across distributed client workloads; led SOC 2 and GxP audit readiness with rigorous control documentation and evidence collection

Led incident response on a live 34-hour Mirai-variant SSH brute-force attack — isolated via Cowrie honeypots and ipset blocklists, performed full network forensics, and submitted structured threat intelligence to CISA and IC3
Deployed Terraform-managed infrastructure and multi-WAN failover firewalls (Cisco, SonicWall, Barracuda); applied STRIDE threat modeling to client architecture reviews and onboarding workflows
Performed SQL Server administration including database migrations during M&A activity, resolving access, policy, and identity conflicts

experience.title: Enterprise Project Manager
experience.description: Directed Fortune 500 infrastructure buildouts and data center migrations for healthcare, financial, and union facilities.
bullets: Delivered a $30M Central DuPage Hospital infrastructure installation under budget and ahead of schedule with full clinical system validation
Executed zero-downtime decommissioning and migration of 560+ legacy servers for an NYSE-listed firm through controlled validation pipelines and phased cutovers
Managed complete infrastructure buildout at IUOE Local 150's Chicago headquarters — firewalls, LAN/WAN design, VoIP, and Cisco network deployments

experience.title: Senior Systems Engineer IV & Global Standards Chair
experience.description: Founded and led the Desktop Architecture team; chaired global standards across 7 business units.
bullets: Founded the Certified Factory Image (CFI) program — a global supply-chain security initiative validating OS images at the factory with Dell, HP, and Lenovo across 100,000+ engineering endpoints; the approach is now standard industry practice
Chaired the Global Hardware Standards Committee; authored enterprise disaster recovery and operational continuity protocols for the WSDD division
Recovered $3M in duplicate vendor charges through meticulous cross-database audits and line-by-line contract review
Promoted to Team Lead, then Asset Manager overseeing 3 storerooms and all tracked hardware across engineering labs

philosophy:
I build security tooling from scratch — not just configure it. The most interesting problems right now live where AI agents meet production infrastructure, and that's exactly where I work.

I break things so you don't have to.
